intro:
I couldn't find information on the plant Lapanthus duartei. However, I found information on Lapachoside-containing plants, specifically Lapachoside-containing Tabebuia spp., which is close. 

Tabebuia avellanedae, also known as Tabebuia chrysotricha, is known as Lapachoside-containing species. 

The Lapachoside-containing Tabebuia avellanedae is a tropical flowering plant native to South America. The health benefits of Tabebuia avellanedae include its anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antibacterial properties.

The bioactive constituents of Lapachoside-containing Tabebuia avellanedae include lapachol, a naphthoquinone with demonstrated antioxidant and anticancer properties, and naphthoquinones, which have been shown to have antiviral and antibacterial effects.

Tabebuia avellanedae is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, capsules, and extracts to take advantage of its health benefits.
